The control grid is the mechanism by which friction between identity, payment, and movement domains collapses into a single addressable surface.
Federal identity standard supplies the digital-ID pillar Fitts identifies.
Local ALPR hardware is the third pillar — taxpayer-funded community-level capture nodes Fitts explicitly names.
Stablecoin legitimation supplies the programmable-money pillar without requiring a formal CBDC.
Removes the last refuge — being deviceless. Identification with zero user action collapses the friction of evading recognition.
Complements facial recognition as a second biometric channel; works where cameras can't, e.g., interiors, around corners, in the dark.
Adds an ambient, invisible identification layer to the synthesis grid — every router a sensor, indoors and out.
Synthesis platforms turn raw BFI identification events into persistent, joinable identity trails across locations.
Palantir is paradigmatic cloud-capital: a fief that rents algorithmic governance to states and firms; technofeudalism names and frames what it is.
Both name a synthesis-layer power structure but from opposed political camps (Varoufakis left / Fitts right) — convergence on diagnosis, divergence on remedy is a pressure point worth tracking.
Frames stablecoin legislation as privatization of the dollar by cloud-capital owners — the monetary arm of techlordism.
AI data center buildout is the physical substrate of cloud capital; without it, the rent-extracting fiefs do not function at scale.
RAGE doctrine reframes career civil servants as an illegitimate 'Cathedral' to be purged, legitimizing definition-of-enemy expansion to bureaucracy itself.
Provides intellectual cover for consolidating coercive authority under a unitary executive bypassing inter-agency norms.
Pressure point: Yarvin openly names what Havel's system relies on obscuring — that the ritual of democratic legitimacy is the target, not an incidental cost.
Cloud-capital concentration produces the CEO-sovereign archetype Yarvin's monarchism rhetorically formalizes.
Land's essay systematized Yarvin/Moldbug's writings into a named doctrine, elevating him from blogger to movement theorist.
Framing democratic opposition as decadent or illegitimate underwrites the expanding definition of internal enemies.
CEO-monarch governance ideal provides ideological cover for cloud-capital concentration as a feature, not a bug.

Pressure point: NRx openly rejects the egalitarian 'ritual' Havel described, but in doing so still requires its own coercive ideological conformity — the same post-totalitarian dynamic in reverse drag.
Directly opposes the transhumanist/posthumanist vision of 'second-class' humans and elite technological dominance Leo names as ideology.
Names concentration of data/algorithms/platforms in private hands as contrary to universal destination of goods; calls for data as common good.
Explicitly identifies algorithmic profiling, social control via data collection, and 'new colonialism' of health/genetic data as grave moral violations.
Notes AI's enormous energy/water demands and ecological cost as incompatible with care for common home — internal tension for any Catholic-affiliated infrastructure buildout.
